quote_of_day.text:
>>7677
Iac-anon is purely Pajeet slop.
Do not compare it to inferior goyslop.
Pajeetslop is better, stronger and more sloppier.
But hey it's OUR slop. Isn't it? So IMO it's better.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of absurd declarations moved over the board, bringing scattered thunderstorms of outlandish threats. Anons made various claims, including one that small Tamil subreddits were run by Pakistanis and Bangladeshis, sparking brief but intense gusts of xenophobic rhetoric.

A heat wave of unapologetic humor swept through the discussion on Indiachan Radio, with one anon praising the station and making light of manifestation and ichor. The conversation remained relatively clear of dense fog, with posters engaging directly on the topics.

A gentle drizzle of personal inquiries fell in the evening, as one anon asked about gay sex experiences.

Nearby, a brief discussion on shilling Indiachan on social media sparked a small cluster of replies, but the topic did not gain significant traction.

articles.title: Anon Praises Indiachan Radio, Discusses Manifestation and Ichor
articles.body:
Anon enthusiastically promoted Indiachan Radio, calling it essential to life, and joking about its deadpan delivery and explosive topics. The original poster expressed amusement at a robotic text-to-speech voice and a topic described as 'so funny'.

Another anon discussed a concept called 'Ichor', a valuable liquid substance that, when taken intravenously, slows down the user's perception of time.

According to this anon, individuals have a 'degree' that measures their ability to fight, with higher degrees allowing for faster subjective time. This supposedly makes guns obsolete.

A different anon provided resources for understanding manifestation, recommending authors like Neville Goddard and Edward Art on YouTube. This anon also suggested looking up 4dbarbie on Tumblr, specifically their posts on Nisargdatta Maharaj.

Anon IAC appeared to be involved in creating content for Indiachan Radio and other automated projects. They appreciated listeners and promoted their work on EOI and the board. The conversation also touched on the idea of manifesting success and changing one's perception.

One anon shared their personal experience of manifesting attractiveness, stating that it worked as if they had already become attractive, and people noticed it more. The thread included various tangents, including a discussion on namefagging and the moderation of other imageboards.

Anon IAC mentioned not needing to namefag, as their presence was recognizable across different monikers. The conversation concluded with some anons exchanging advice on creative projects and publishing.

One anon advised getting something out quickly rather than waiting for a big book, suggesting formats like audio dramas or animated YouTube series.

articles.title: Anons Make Outlandish Declarations and Threats
articles.body:
Anon started a thread with the incomplete phrase 'We the'. One anon responded by declaring that low-IQ individuals disrupting the constitution-writing process would be forced to perform a specific, humiliating act on 'andamani tribals'.

Another anon described a person with specific physical characteristics relative to a 'pajeet'. Anons made various outlandish declarations, including that 'every gobar will be slave of jeets' and 'every pajeet is promised' unspecified things.

Some anons referenced Instagram and proposed punishments for users. The thread included graphic and disturbing content, such as an anon stating that certain individuals 'will be used as foods' and another describing a violent sexual act.

Anons also made references to tattoos and physical mutilation. Anons continued to make bizarre announcements, including that 'moreover, every UC will be given' unspecified access and 'access to top fertile gorimem'.

The thread contained numerous references to other posts, but their content was not specified.

articles.title: Anon asks about gay sex experiences
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread asking if anyone who has had gay sex as a bottom would share their experience.

One respondent claimed to have had the experience and said it could be great if one can enjoy it, comparing its pleasure to heterosexual sex. This anon advised ensuring relaxation of the anus before sex to avoid pain.

Another anon questioned the original poster's motives, suggesting they were the same person who had been hating on women in a previous thread and defending Dalits in the morning.

Some anons called for the removal of what they termed 'gay faggotry' and 'troony/faggy shit' from the board, with one stating that such posts should be removed unless they are 'cute'.

An admin's advice not to remove posts unless they violate rules was referenced by one anon. The idea of a separate forum for such topics was floated, with one anon joking that such a space already exists, referencing '/b/' and 'plebbit'.

A direct question about whether the board is pro-LGBT was asked by another anon.

articles.title: Anon Claims Small Tamil Subreddits Run by Pakistanis, Bangladeshis
articles.body:
Anon posted that even small Tamil subreddits are now run by Pakistanis and Bangladeshis, claiming that you can't trust anyone. This led to various responses about misinformation and racial stereotyping online.

Another anon noted that people often misattribute nationalities to individuals based on appearance, citing examples of good-looking Indian Hindus being labeled as Pakistani, Afghan, or Persian, and ugly Sri Lankans, Pakistanis, or Bangladeshis being labeled as Indian.

Anon also mentioned that Reddit is used to spread 'psyops content' and that urban Indians use it, posing a threat to the country.

Some respondents made derogatory comments about other groups, with one anon suggesting that North Indian Aryans have to suffer because of South Indian Dravidians. Others made incendiary remarks about genocide and racial superiority.

articles.title: Anons discuss shilling Indiachan on social media
articles.body: Anon started a thread saying 'let's shill indiachan.' One anon replied with a new Twitter account, claiming to have thousands of followers, and asked others to follow them. Some anons expressed skepticism about shilling on Twitter, citing previous failures. One suggested shilling on 'neutral spaces' like anime, gaming, or sports subreddits, excluding 'Librandu Dalits.' Anons also discussed technical issues, including admin blocking major crawlers hurting SEO visibility.
